breaking.text: Dow futures slip ~270 points overnight as post-rally momentum fades ahead of jobless claims data
Broadcom surges 5% after hours on record $19.3B Q1 revenue, projects $100B AI chip revenue by 2027
Treasury Sec. Bessent confirms 15% global tariff takes effect this week, replacing prior 10% rate under Section 122

summary: Wall Street rallied Wednesday as easing oil prices and solid economic data offset Middle East tensions, with the S&P 500 gaining 0.78% to 6,869.50, the Nasdaq surging 1.29%, and the Dow adding 238 points. Crypto-linked stocks exploded higher — Coinbase jumped 14% and MicroStrategy rose 10% — after President Trump signaled support for the stablecoin Clarity Act and Bitcoin reclaimed $73,000. After the close, Broadcom delivered a blockbuster Q1 earnings beat with record $19.3 billion revenue and guided Q2 to $22 billion, while CEO Hock Tan projected AI chip revenue exceeding $100 billion in 2027. Thursday brings key labor data including initial jobless claims, revised Q4 productivity, and Challenger layoff figures ahead of Friday's pivotal nonfarm payrolls report.

highlights.text: Thursday's economic calendar heavy with labor data: Challenger job cuts, initial jobless claims, revised Q4 productivity and unit labor costs — all ahead of Friday's nonfarm payrolls
Cracker Barrel pops 9% after-hours on surprise Q2 earnings beat: EPS of $0.25 vs consensus loss of -$0.20, revenue $874.8M tops estimates
Moderna rallies nearly 11% after agreeing to pay up to $2.25 billion to settle Covid vaccine patent dispute with Biopharma Corp and Genevant Sciences
Coinbase surges 14% and MicroStrategy jumps 10% as Bitcoin reclaims $73,000 on Trump administration support for stablecoin Clarity Act legislation
Broadcom posts record Q1 revenue of $19.3B (+29% YoY), AI revenue soars 106% to $8.4B; CEO projects $100B+ AI chip revenue in 2027 with OpenAI as new sixth customer
Key economic data due March 5: Initial Jobless Claims (8:30 AM), Q4 revised Unit Labor Costs & Nonfarm Productivity, and Challenger Job Cuts report
StubHub posts $1.9B net loss in first post-IPO earnings on $1.4B stock-based compensation charge, but guides FY2026 to $9.9-10.1B GMS and $400-420M adjusted EBITDA
Treasury Secretary Bessent says 15% global tariffs under Section 122 take effect this week, but predicts rates return to pre-Supreme Court ruling levels within 5 months
Veeva Systems beats Q4 estimates with $836M revenue (+13% YoY) and $2.06 EPS vs $1.93 consensus, maintaining 40%+ operating margins — shares up 4% after hours
Broadcom CEO Hock Tan outlines path to $100B in AI chip revenue, citing progress with Anthropic, Meta, and OpenAI as key custom accelerator customers
